name: PALISADE BIO, INC.
business: Palisade Bio, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing next-generation, once-daily, oral phosphodiesterase-4 (PDE4) inhibitor prodrugs designed for targeted delivery to the terminal ileum and colon. Its lead clinical product candidate, PALI-2108, is being developed as a treatment for patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). The company is also advancing an exploratory Phase 1b cohort in fibrostenotic Crohn's disease (FSCD).

exposures.sentence: Our lead clinical product candidate, PALI-2108, is being developed as a treatment for patients living with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD).

We are developing a biomarker-based patient selection approach that we believe may aid clinicians in identifying patients who may better respond to PALI-2108, thereby improving the rate of clinical response previously demonstrated with PDE4 inhibitors.

catalysts.event: First patients dosed in exploratory Phase 1b cohort in FSCD
catalysts.sentence: On October 16, 2025, we dosed our first patients in an exploratory Phase 1b cohort in FSCD while we complete longer-term chronic safety and toxicology studies.

catalysts.event: Positive results announced from the UC cohort portion of the Phase 1 study
catalysts.sentence: On May 27, 2025, we announced positive results from the SAD, MAD and FE cohorts in healthy volunteers and on August 7, 2025 and September 17, 2025, we announced positive results from the UC cohort portion of the study.

catalysts.event: IND submission for Phase 2 UC study
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate data from the exploratory Phase 1b cohort in FSCD together with results from the Phase 1/1b UC program will support Investigational New Drug Application (IND) submissions to the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for a Phase 2 UC study in the second quarter of 2026 and a Phase 2 CD study in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: IND submission for Phase 2 CD study
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate data from the exploratory Phase 1b cohort in FSCD together with results from the Phase 1/1b UC program will support Investigational New Drug Application (IND) submissions to the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for a Phase 2 UC study in the second quarter of 2026 and a Phase 2 CD study in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase 2 primary efficacy readout for UC
catalysts.sentence: Management believes the October 2025 Offering for net proceeds of $127.6 million will provide sufficient capital to fund our operations through major clinical development milestones including a Phase 2 primary efficacy readout of PALI-2108 for UC that is expected in the second half of 2027 and a Phase 2 primary efficacy readout of PALI-2108 for CD that is expected in 2028.

catalysts.event: Phase 2 primary efficacy readout for CD
catalysts.sentence: Management believes the October 2025 Offering for net proceeds of $127.6 million will provide sufficient capital to fund our operations through major clinical development milestones including a Phase 2 primary efficacy readout of PALI-2108 for UC that is expected in the second half of 2027 and a Phase 2 primary efficacy readout of PALI-2108 for CD that is expected in 2028.

tamClaims.claim: The market for 8MM for UC treatments was valued at approximately $7.3 billion in 2021 and is expected to grow to over $9.5 billion in 2031 at a compound annual growth rate of approximately 2.78%.
tamClaims.sentence: The market for 8MM for UC treatments was valued at approximately $7.3 billion in 2021 and is expected to grow to over $9.5 billion in 2031 at a compound annual growth rate ('CAGR') of approximately 2.78%.

tamClaims.claim: The global market for CD treatments was valued at $13.9 billion in 2022 and is projected to grow to approximately $25.5 billion in 2032 at a CAGR of approximately 6%.
tamClaims.sentence: The global market for CD treatments was valued at $13.9 billion in 2022 and is projected to grow to approximately $25.5 billion in 2032 at a CAGR of approximately 6%.
